metadata.notes:
agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full; driver_capability: gap:wait_turn

assessment.body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full; driver_capability: gap:wait_turn

## Reasoning

The scenario's two acceptance bullets are the canonical lifecycle baseline: `ready â†’ working â†’ ready` and `TotalTokens > 0` after the turn.

1. **agent_supports = yes.** Mistral Vibe is an interactive coding REPL that does exactly this round-trip on every prompt. Confirmed against live transcripts under `~/.vibe/logs/session/<session_...>/messages.jsonl` (CLI v2.19.0): each session opens with a `role:user` prompt line and the model answers with `role:assistant` lines â€” a text-only terminal assistant message closes the turn. No feature flag or mode is required; a bare prompt exercises the arc.

2. **daemon_capability = full.** The vibe adapter (`core/adapters/inbound/agents/vibe/`) tails `messages.jsonl` via `FilesUnderRoot` and derives the state arc in `parser.go`: `role:user` â†’ `user_message` (opens the turn â†’ working); `role:assistant` WITH `tool_calls[]` â†’ `assistant_message` (stays working); a text-only `role:assistant` (no tool_calls) â†’ `turn_done` (settles â†’ ready). `role:tool` â†’ `tool_result` linked by `tool_call_id`. This maps the full `ready â†’ working â†’ ready` topology with zero tool calls for the minimal prompt. The `TotalTokens > 0` bullet is also observable: `applySidecar` reads `stats.context_tokens` from the sibling `meta.json` and, on `turn_done`, emits `ev.Tokens = &TokenSnapshot{Total: context_tokens}`. Live sidecars carry non-zero `context_tokens` (e.g. 136662), so `tokens_nonzero=true` is a real, emitted observation.

3. **driver_capability = gap:wait_turn.** The lifecycle recipe must block for turn completion before settling, so it needs a `wait_turn` step. The freshly-scaffolded `replaydata/agents/mistral-vibe/driver-interactive.sh` declares `DRIVE_ELICITS="send slash sleep"` and its `wait_turn()` arm is a `not_implemented` stub (SEAM 2), plus the launch-binary and transcript-path seams (1/3) are unfilled. `recipe_semantic_gaps` (tools/onboarding-factory/scripts/lib/recipe-lint.sh) flags any recipe using `wait_turn` as a semantic_gap because it is not in DRIVE_ELICITS. This is TOOLING work, not an observability limit â€” the recipe and spec are real; `record` ports `wait_turn` (and SEAMs 1/3) from a reference driver (e.g. drive-kiro-cli / drive-codex) before it drives.

## Model / cost / tokens

Model id `mistral-medium-3.5` confirmed from the live surface: `config.active_model` in every recent `meta.json` sidecar. `NormalizeModelName` returns it unchanged (no claude/date pattern matches), so the daemon displays and the observations pin `mistral-medium-3.5`. Tokens: non-zero (sidecar `context_tokens` â†’ turn_done TokenSnapshot). Cost: NOT emitted â€” `sidecar.go` reads only `working_directory`, `active_model`, and `context_tokens`; it does NOT read `stats.session_cost`, and mistral-vibe is absent from `core/pkg/capacity` (context-WINDOW unresolved; model NAME still displays). So `cost_nonzero=false`.

## Birth model (provisional)

Single-birth: the session id is the `session_<...>` directory (`sessionIDFromPath`), a stable id from launch. Modeled like claudecode/kiro-cli's settled shape â€” birth anchored to `start` unpinned. Whether a transient `proc-<PID>` presession row appears before the transcript is emission-dependent and cannot be confirmed from source; `record` settles it (kiro-cli's assessment made the same assumption and the recording refuted a presession row).
caveats: driver=gap:wait_turn: driver-interactive.sh is a fresh scaffold â€” wait_turn is a not_implemented stub and SEAMs 1 (launch binary) and 3 (transcript-path resolution) are unfilled. record ports these from a reference driver before recording.

Birth model (single-birth, start-anchored) and the absence of a proc-<PID> presession row are PROVISIONAL â€” they depend on emission the source can't confirm; record promotes them from provisional to settled.

cost_nonzero=false: the sidecar carries stats.session_cost but the parser does not read it, and mistral-vibe is absent from core/pkg/capacity, so no per-turn cost or context-window is emitted (model NAME still displays).

sources.note: userâ†’user_message (working); assistant+tool_callsâ†’assistant_message; text-only assistantâ†’turn_done (ready); toolâ†’tool_result. applySidecar emits Tokens.Total=context_tokens on turn_done.
meta.json sidecar reads working_directory, config.active_model, stats.context_tokens only â€” session_cost is NOT read, so no cost is emitted.
DRIVE_ELICITS="send slash sleep"; wait_turn() is a not_implemented stub (SEAM 2); launch/transcript-path SEAMs 1/3 unfilled â†’ recipe's wait_turn is a semantic_gap.
Live sidecar (vibe 2.19.0): config.active_model=mistral-medium-3.5, stats.context_tokens=136662 (non-zero), stats.session_cost present but unread by the adapter.
NormalizeModelName returns mistral-medium-3.5 unchanged (no matching alias/date/claude pattern), so the displayed + pinned model id is mistral-medium-3.5.

preconditions: mistral-vibe (vibe) v2.19.0 on PATH and authenticated (Mistral API auth persisted on this machine)
irrlichd is recording â€” run-cell.sh in isolated mode, or --attach against a daemon started with --record
no other vibe session shares the same cwd (PID is bound by cwd)
setup: Create an empty cwd under <staging>/cwd/ (the driver launches vibe there); the session_<...> transcript dir is minted under ~/.vibe/logs/session/

script.text: Reply with exactly the word: ok

verify: events.jsonl mints the session_<...> session in ready shortly after launch (single-birth; id from the transcript directory)
events.jsonl contains a pid_discovered event binding the vibe PID to the session (matched by cwd)
events.jsonl contains a state_transition to working when the user prompt lands
events.jsonl contains a state_transition to ready after the text-only assistant reply (turn_done)
the turn_done event carries Tokens.Total > 0 (from sidecar context_tokens)
